read.thesis: The psychology of evil is not cruelty by itself. In Jiang's reconstruction, evil is what happens when power learns to produce a person who can act without feeling: the ruler becomes unpredictable, stress-proof, and without empathy; myth becomes a script for installing selves; trauma, ritual, scent, and memory become control channels; and the War on Terror becomes a modern repetition of the same pharaoh-making machine. The lecture's most important sentence is also its warning label: this is speculation, and it must be read as a theory with source pressure, not as settled history.

opening.heading: Core Reading
opening.text: The lecture begins with a metaphysical reversal. The old world says mind leads to matter: the universe is a giant mind vibrating, and matter appears where the vibrations intersect. Modern school says the opposite: the brain creates the mind, only the measurable exists, and the eye must surrender to data, tools, and theory. That reversal matters because the whole lecture is about control. If mind can shape matter, then controlling experience, memory, ritual, and identity becomes a political weapon. Secret societies' alleged secret weapon is not a symbol. It is a programmable human being.

beats.heading: Mass Society Needs Control
beats.summary: The oldest civilizations are introduced as four social-control machines: bureaucracy, religion, war, and the god-pharaoh.

paragraphs.text: A mass society first has to solve one problem: how do you get everyone to obey, coordinate, and not tear the whole thing apart? China answers with bureaucracy, with the keju focusing family ambition into the state. The Indus Valley answers with a peaceful egalitarian religion that Jiang treats as a prototype of Buddhism. Mesopotamia answers with war because it has no natural defenses. Egypt answers with the pharaoh, the man who is not just king but God.

beats.heading: The Modern Script
beats.summary: The War on Terror is pulled into the Egyptian frame: Abu Ghraib, learned helplessness, ISIS, Guantanamo, and MK Ultra become modern pharaoh-making.

paragraphs.text: The modern comparison begins at Abu Ghraib. The official story says rogue soldiers went crazy. Jiang's version says look at the psychologists, the CIA payments, and the program design. Enhanced interrogation is then mapped onto the mythic identities: learned helplessness is an attempt to turn Ra, the active fighter, into Osiris, the passive victim.

beats.heading: You Are Now The Pharaoh

paragraphs.text: The punchline is almost comic and not comic at all: good news, you are now the pharaoh. The results of MK Ultra, Jiang says, have spread through social media, drug regimes, psychology, mass education, and mass culture. The old ritual chamber becomes the whole environment. The control surface is no longer only the throne room. It is the feed, the classroom, the clinic, the cable, the mood.

source_notes.heading: Attribution And Speculation
source_notes.text: This read preserves Jiang's dated 2025-09-11 lecture model. Claims about Egyptian priestly programming, secret societies, dissociation, torture programs, MK Ultra, ISIS, psychology, and social media are presented as the lecture's speculative reconstruction and interpretation, not as independently established factual reporting. The source itself repeatedly tells students to doubt the Egyptian theory and take it skeptically.
